column10: Returning user login via mobile + OTP. Gateway to the main app.

column5: Login screen displays all required UI elements
column11:
1. Look at the full screen from top to bottom.
2. Confirm you see: Apna Rojgar branding/logo, mobile number input field, country code (+91), a 'Send OTP' button, a link for New Registration, and optionally a Change Language link.
3. Do not enter any data yet — this test is only about visibility.
column12:
1. Mobile number field is visible and tappable.
2. Country code +91 is shown next to the mobile field.
3. Send OTP button is visible and enabled.
4. New Registration link is visible.
5. No blank white screen, crash, or missing translation keys (e.g. 'auth.login.title').

column5: Send OTP succeeds for a valid registered mobile number
column10: Logged out · Use a mobile number that is already registered on the staging server (use team test data)
column11:
1. Tap the mobile number field and type a valid 10-digit registered number.
2. Tap the 'Send OTP' button.
3. Wait up to 30 seconds and watch for: a loading indicator, a success toast/message, and the OTP input field appearing.
4. Check your SMS inbox (staging: 000000) — an OTP message should arrive.
column12:
1. Send OTP button shows loading state while request is in progress.
2. A success message or toast appears (e.g. 'OTP sent successfully').
3. OTP input field(s) appear on the same screen — user stays on Login.
4. No error toast saying 'user not found' for a valid registered number.
5. SMS OTP is received on the test device (or use dev OTP 000000 on staging).

column5: Correct OTP logs in a user with complete profile and opens Home
column10: Logged out · OTP has been sent to a registered user with a COMPLETE profile (name, address, age, gender all filled). OTP field is visible on Login screen
column11:
1. After OTP is sent, enter the 6-digit OTP received via SMS (on staging/dev you may use 000000).
2. Tap Verify / Login / Submit button.
3. Wait for navigation — the app should transition away from Login.
4. Confirm you see the bottom tab bar with 5 tabs (Home, Work, People, Activity, Profile).
column12:
1. OTP is accepted without error.
2. App navigates to the main app (/(tabs)) — NOT stuck on Login.
3. Bottom navigation bar with 5 tabs is visible.
4. Home tab is selected by default.
5. User remains logged in if app is killed and reopened (session persisted).

column5: Dev bypass OTP 000000 works on staging
column10: Logged out · Staging/dev environment only (staging API). OTP already sent
column11:
1. Send OTP to any valid registered test number.
2. When OTP field appears, type 000000 (six zeros).
3. Tap Verify / Login.
4. Observe whether login succeeds.
column12:
1. Login succeeds on staging/dev without needing real SMS.
2. User navigates to main tabs.
3. This OTP must NOT work on production — if it does, report as a security bug.
column14: Dev bypass is in utils/devOtp.ts — staging only.

column5: Wrong OTP shows clear error and keeps user on Login
column10: Logged out · OTP has been sent. OTP input field is visible
column11:
1. Send OTP to a valid number.
2. Enter an intentionally wrong OTP.
3. Tap Verify / Login.
4. Read any error message shown.
column12:
1. An error message appears (e.g. 'Invalid OTP' or similar in Hindi/English).
2. User remains on the Login screen — NOT navigated to Home.
3. OTP field is cleared or editable so user can retry.
4. App does not crash.

column5: Resend OTP delivers a new code after timer expires
column10: Logged out · OTP has been sent. Wait for the Resend OTP countdown timer to finish (usually 30–60 seconds)
column11:
1. Send OTP and wait until the 'Resend OTP' button becomes tappable (countdown ends).
2. Tap 'Resend OTP'.
3. Wait for confirmation message.
4. Enter the NEW OTP (not the old one) and submit.
column12:
1. Resend button is disabled during countdown, then becomes active.
2. Tapping Resend shows success feedback.
3. New OTP is accepted.
4. old OTP is rejected.
5. Login completes successfully with the new OTP.

column5: Invalid mobile number is rejected before OTP is sent
column10: Logged out · On Login screen. No OTP sent yet
column11:
1. Test A: Enter only 5 digits and tap Send OTP.
2. Test B: Enter letters or special characters and tap Send OTP.
3. Test C: Leave the field empty and tap Send OTP.
column12:
1. For all three tests: OTP is NOT sent.
2. A validation error message appears near the mobile field.
3. OTP input field does NOT appear.
4. User stays on Login screen.

column5: Job detail page loads with all information sections
column10: Any test account · At least one active job exists on staging. Log in as any role. Know a valid job ID (tap any job card from Home or Work tab)

1. From Home or Work tab, tap any job/service card to open its detail page.
2. Wait for the page to fully load (spinner disappears).
3. Slowly scroll from top to bottom and look for each section listed in Expected Result.
4. Take note if any section is missing or shows an error.
column12:
1. Job photo carousel/gallery is visible at the top.
2. Job title, category, and daily pay rate are shown.
3. Requirements section shows workers needed per skill.
4. Facilities section shows food/living/travel/ESI-PF if employer added them.
5. Employer info box shows employer name and photo.
6. Map or location section is visible.
7. Action buttons at bottom match your role (Apply for Worker, Manage for Employer).

column5: Worker can apply to an open job
column10: Worker account · Log in as Worker. Open a job you have NOT applied to yet. Worker profile should have matching skills (or be ready to add skill in modal)
column11:
1. Log in as Worker and open a job detail page you have not applied to.
2. Scroll to the bottom action area and find the 'Apply' button.
3. Tap Apply. If a confirmation dialog or skill modal appears, complete it.
4. Wait for the API response (loading indicator on button).
5. Observe how the Apply button changes after success.
column12:
1. Apply button shows loading state while submitting.
2. Success toast appears (e.g. 'Application submitted').
3. Button changes to 'Applied' or 'Cancel Apply' state.
4. Job appears in Activity tab → Applied Jobs sub-tab.
5. Employer can see this worker in the Applicants list for that job.

column5: Worker can cancel a pending job application
column10: Worker account · Log in as Worker. Open a job you HAVE already applied to
column11:
1. Log in as Worker and open a job you previously applied to.
2. Find the 'Cancel Apply' or similar button (replaces Apply after applying).
3. Tap Cancel Apply and confirm if a dialog appears.
4. Check Activity tab → Applied Jobs to verify removal.
column12:
1. Cancel action succeeds with confirmation toast.
2. Button reverts to 'Apply' state.
3. Job is removed from Activity → Applied Jobs list.
4. Employer no longer sees this worker in Applicants (or status shows cancelled).

column5: Employer sees Applications and Selections tabs on own job
column10: Employer account · Log in as Employer. Open a job YOU posted (Activity → My Services, or your own listing)
column11:
1. Log in as Employer and navigate to a job you posted.
2. Scroll down looking for tab bar or sections labelled 'Applications' and 'Selections'.
3. Tap each tab and observe the content.
column12:
1. Applications tab is visible on employer's own job.
2. Selections tab is visible next to Applications.
3. Applications tab lists workers who applied (or shows empty state).
4. Selections tab lists workers employer has selected (or empty state).
5. These tabs are NOT shown when viewing another employer's job.
